regime_justification: USDJPY 159.19, 55 pips below reported April/May intervention threshold of 160; JGB 10Y at 2.78%, near 30-year highs; BOJ April meeting held at 0.75% but hawkish dissents (3 board members wanted 1.0%); MOF/BOJ messaging cautious on FX volatility; US-Iran progress rumors capping oil slightly but ¥/bbl remains ~50% above pre-conflict; carry spread intact but vol subdued—intervention risk elevated but not imminent.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY grinding just below 160 watch line after reported May 1 intervention; JGB 10Y 2.78% (30-yr high); BOJ rhetoric shifting hawkish (3 dissents at April MPM); oil ~$97/bbl keeping ¥/bbl elevated; spread 187 bps still wide but JGB bear steepening accelerating—classic Stage 1.

1w.label: Unlikely
1w.driver: USDJPY 55 pips below watch line, VIX 16.7, MOVE 78.4, oil pulling back on Iran talk optimism, next BOJ meeting Jun 15-16 (3 weeks out), vol subdued—minor pressure contained.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D. Setup fragile: JGB at 30-yr high, hawkish BOJ dissent on record, MOF 2 intervention 'windows' left by Nov per IMF rules, US-Iran deal uncertainty could re-spike oil. Carry intact but basis/vol coiling.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Jun 15-16 BOJ meeting inside horizon; market pricing ~40% chance of hike to 1.0% if oil stabilizes. Strong GDP Q1, wage momentum, and 3 dissents at April meeting suggest board primed. Meeting could catalyze JPY strength or disappointment selloff.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If Jun BOJ delivers hike as priced, risk-off pressure dissipates; if hold, USDJPY grinds higher but orderly. US yields likely stable absent new Iran shock. By week 4, current setup either resolves (hike) or extends (grind)—non-monotonic decay from week 3.

bottom_line: Carry regime under strain but intact. USDJPY 159.19, 55 pips shy of MOF's 160 watch line; JGB 10Y 2.78% (30-yr high) pressuring yen; BOJ Apr meeting saw 3 hawkish dissents wanting 1.0%. Oil ~$97 (+50% pre-war) keeping ¥/bbl imported inflation elevated despite US-Iran talk optimism. Spread 187 bps wide, but vol compressed (USDJPY 1M IV data stale, VIX 16.7, MOVE 78.4). Stage 1: pressure building, not yet pre-intervention. Jun 15-16 BOJ meeting is the next binary. Crypto: BTC $76.8k stable, XRP $1.36 weak (-4% 7d)—risk-on rotation muted, no liquidity tailwind. Grade 1w D (unlikely), 2w/3w C (watchful, Jun BOJ catalyst), 4w D (resolution or extension). Prior applied week 2 (ambiguous vol setup). Watch: USDJPY pace, JGB 2.80% breach, BOJ June dot shift, Iran deal headlines.

trigger_map.note: Data unavailable; last available (Apr) showed IV subdued ~7.6% annualized—no skew flip signal yet

narrative.p1: USDJPY printed 159.19 on May 24, up +0.13% from Friday's close, grinding 55 pips below the reported 160 intervention threshold that triggered MOF action in late April/early May. Oil trimmed gains to $96.60 (+0.3%) on renewed US-Iran talk optimism—Rubio cited 'slight progress'—but remains ~50% above pre-war levels. JGB 10Y eased 1 bp to 2.78%, still at 30-year highs, 2 bps shy of the Sept 1996 cycle peak of 2.80%. US 10Y fell 3 bps to 4.56% on the Iran headlines, widening the carry spread to ~187 bps (using recent 4.65% reading, corrected from earlier 4.56%). No BOJ/MOF headlines in the last 24 hours; last relevant event was May 12 Katayama-Bessent meeting discussing FX market implications of Middle East situation. Regime unchanged from yesterday's yellow; still Stage 1 (Pressure Building)—carry intact but JGB bear steepening accelerating, USDJPY pace slow but directionally higher, BOJ hawkish dissent on record.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 187 bps (using 4.65%-2.78%); carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (USDJPY 1M IV data stale, last read ~7.6% in late April per forex.com archive—if that holds, carry/vol ~24.6, elevated but not extreme). USDJPY 5d +0.26%—pace slow, well below the multi-percent daily moves that historically trigger MOF rate-checks. USDJPY distance from 60d mean: trading ~1.5% above 60d SMA per chart inference (157.5-158 zone), moderately stretched but not dislocated. ¥/bbl = $96.60 × 159.19 = ¥15,378/bbl, down slightly from peaks but still pressuring imported inflation. Stage 1 justified: BOJ April minutes (released May 7) showed members urging rate hikes 'without long intervals' if oil persists; April decision held 0.75% but saw 3 dissents (Takata, Tamura, Nakagawa) wanting 1.0%—hawkish skew building. JGB 10Y at 2.78% reflects fiscal concerns (PM Takaichi urged supplementary budget for commodity costs) and normalization bets. VIX 16.7, MOVE 78.4—vol subdued across assets, classic late-cycle complacency. Grades 1w D: setup contained, no imminent catalyst, USDJPY below watch line, next BOJ 3 weeks out. Grade 2w C: **applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D**. Data ambiguous—vol compressed, spread wide, but JGB at 30-yr high, 3 BOJ dissents, MOF only 2 intervention windows left by Nov per IMF rules (May 5 official comments), US-Iran deal fragile (Supreme Leader hardening uranium stance per Reuters May 22). The prior tips C because the carry regime is set up for a fast unwind if a catalyst hits (BOJ surprise hike, Iran shock, JGB 2.80% break), even though base case is grind. Grade 3w C: Jun 15-16 BOJ meeting is binary—market pricing ~40% hike to 1.0% if oil stabilizes (per Apr Outlook inflation forecast raised to 2.8% from 1.9%). If hike, JPY strengthens, unwind risk spikes short-term. If hold, disappointment could push USDJPY toward 160+. Grade 4w D: non-monotonic—by week 4, Jun BOJ outcome either resolves pressure (hike = tightening priced, risk-off fades) or extends grind (hold = carry grinds higher but orderly). Current 3-week spike in risk reflects the meeting itself.
narrative.p3: BTC $76.8k, up +2.8% on the day but range-bound $75k-$78k over the past week—no breakout, consolidation mode. XRP $1.36, up +4.0% today but down -3.8% over 7 days, underperforming BTC and showing rotation fragility. BTC open interest direction unavailable in pulls, but May 22 headlines noted 6 straight days of US spot BTC ETF outflows totaling $1.26B—institutional risk-off in crypto. BTC-DXY 30d correlation unavailable, but DXY 99.32 (+0.08% Friday)—modest dollar strength not driving BTC directionally, suggesting decoupling or range-trade. Crypto liquidity read: muted. BTC stable but not expanding, XRP weak, ETF outflows continuing. No tailwind from risk-on rotation; if USDJPY carry unwinds (Stage 3+), expect BTC to drain on deleveraging (Stage 4 playbook). Current positioning: BTC holding $75k support, but no conviction bid. For crypto longs, the setup is defensive—carry grind intact means crypto can grind sideways to up, but any BOJ/MOF shock flips that fast. Watch Jun 15-16 BOJ: hike = risk-off = BTC tests $70k; hold + USDJPY rally = short-term relief but sets up bigger unwind later. XRP weakness vs BTC suggests altcoin froth gone—flight to BTC quality already underway at the margin.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 D (unlikely) because USDJPY 55 pips below MOF watch line, oil pulling back on Iran talk, vol subdued (VIX 16.7, MOVE 78.4), next BOJ 3 weeks out—no imminent trigger. Week 2 C (watchful) because by day 8-14, macro setup ambiguous: JGB at 30-yr high could break 2.80%, US-Iran talks fragile (could collapse, re-spiking oil), BOJ hawkish dissent on record (3 wanted 1.0% in April), and MOF only has 2 intervention windows left by Nov per IMF freely-floating rules—if USDJPY grinds above 160 in week 2, intervention odds jump. Applied unwind prior to tip C; straight data read would be D (carry intact, vol low), but the *setup* is fragile enough that a tiebreaker leans watchful.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 C to week 3 C: both watchful, but week 3 risk higher because Jun 15-16 BOJ meeting enters the window. By day 15-21, market will front-run BOJ decision—if oil stays elevated (~$95-100) and wages/CPI data (Japan releases early Jun) stay firm, hike odds rise from current ~40% toward 60-70%. That speculation alone can compress USDJPY (JPY strength on hike pricing) or spike it (if market decides BOJ will wimp out). Week 3 also brings G7 Finance Ministers meeting (late May per Katayama-Bessent statement)—potential for coordinated FX messaging or lack thereof. Catalyst density higher in week 3, justifying sustained C despite nograde escalation.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 C to week 4 D: non-monotonic drop reflects Jun BOJ resolution. By day 22-28, the meeting has happened (Jun 15-16). Base case: if BOJ hikes to 1.0%, initial risk-off spike (week 3 tail into early week 4) but then stabilization as market reprices tightening path—unwind pressure eases because policy is adjusting (classic Stage 1 → Stage 0 backtrack if hike is orderly). If BOJ holds at 0.75%, USDJPY likely rallies toward 162-165 (carry grind resumes), but *orderly*—no shock, just grind, so week 4 unwind risk drops back to D (unlikely in 7-day forward window from day 22). The week 3 C captures the meeting itself as binary risk; week 4 D assumes the binary resolves without forcing a full Stage 3 shock. If BOJ surprises ultra-hawkish (1.25% or aggressive dots), week 4 could be B, but that's <10% scenario—median path is hike-to-1.0-and-pause or hold-and-grind, both of which lower 4-week-forward unwind odds.

watch_list: USDJPY daily close above 160.00 (triggers MOF rate-check risk, potential intervention)
JGB 10Y break above 2.80% (30-yr cycle high breach = fiscal/normalization panic accelerates)
US-Iran talks headlines: collapse = oil re-spikes to $110+, ¥/bbl shock; deal = oil sub-$90, eases BOJ inflation pressure
BOJ June meeting pricing shift: if hike odds move >60% before Jun 15, USDJPY front-runs lower; if <30%, grinds higher
MOVE index close above 85 (rates vol uptick = first sign carry regime stress building)
